project: Mount and Blade Style Multi-Party Campaign Flow
overview: Define and deliver an existing-codebase Bevy campaign loop centered on multi-party overworld control, deterministic progression, and a clear player journey from start menu to character creation to overworld, region, and local intro scene.
goals: Make multi-party overworld management the primary gameplay loop before tactical depth expansion
Provide a coherent first-run journey: main menu -> new campaign -> character creation -> overworld -> region -> local intro
Preserve deterministic behavior across campaign transitions, party simulation, and save/load
Improve UX clarity for campaign control handoff, region targeting, and order feedback
nonGoals: Modding systems or public mod APIs
Large-scale content expansion beyond systems needed for the core loop
Comprehensive long-tail economy balancing beyond foundational logistics behavior
successMetrics: A new player can complete the full onboarding flow from start menu to local intro without debug shortcuts
Player can select, focus, and issue delegated orders to multiple parties with visible order state and movement outcomes
Campaign save/load resumes correctly at all implemented layers with no data loss for party state and character creation choices
Deterministic tests validate seeded flow transitions and party simulation repeatability under the same seed
openQuestions: How much true camera continuity is feasible between overworld, region, and local scenes before performance or complexity tradeoffs outweigh UX gains?
What thresholds should trigger party notices so feedback remains informative without becoming noisy?

stack.database: File-based save JSON managed in application persistence
stack.auth: No user auth required (single local profile/session)

routes.name: Start Menu
routes.purpose: Entry point with New Campaign and Continue Campaign actions and collapsed save diagnostics

routes.name: Character Creation - Faction
routes.purpose: Select faction with explicit gameplay effects

routes.name: Character Creation - Backstory
routes.purpose: Select backstory/archetype with explicit stat and recruit-bias effects

routes.name: Overworld Map
routes.purpose: Primary strategic layer for multi-party selection, focus, and delegated orders

routes.name: Region View
routes.purpose: Zoomed regional layer with selected region context and encounter readiness

routes.name: Local Eagle-Eye Intro
routes.purpose: Local scene bootstrap beginning with player character exiting dilapidated structure

uiNotes: Start menu must keep New Campaign as dominant CTA and avoid in-session notice leakage into menu subtitle text.
Overworld party panel must show selected party, commanded party, current order, target region, and latest party notice.
Command handoff UI must distinguish directly controlled party from delegated parties at all times.
Region target assignment should use explicit picker interaction (map click + assign) with clear confirm/cancel states.
Each major screen requires explicit UI states: loading, ready, action-applied, and failure-with-recovery messaging.

importFormat.description: Not applicable for this iteration; game state is authored in runtime and serialized by save/load systems.

rules: New campaign bootstrap must initialize deterministic seed and create party state before entering overworld.
Player character is represented as a hero companion and tracked by `playerHeroId`.
Delegated parties execute order-driven movement periodically when not directly controlled.
Continue Campaign must attempt latest compatible save/autosave and provide failure status with attempted save labels.
First-time launch enforces New Campaign path unless runtime dev mode explicitly bypasses.
Faction and backstory choices apply gameplay effects, including recruit generation bias.
Transitions between overworld, region, and local layers must preserve selected region context and deterministic seed usage.
Debug navigation remains runtime dev-mode gated, not compile-time only.

phasedImplementation.name: Multi-Party Foundation
scope: Party state model (`CampaignParties`, `CampaignParty`, order kinds)
New-campaign party bootstrap (player party + delegated party)
Party save/load persistence
Delegated periodic order execution scaffold
Baseline overworld party panel and order controls

phasedImplementation.name: Party Control UX
scope: Clear selected vs directly controlled party markers
Command handoff (`Take Command`) and focus sync
Explicit delegated target assignment workflow improvements
Next: true map-click target picker with confirm/cancel states

phasedImplementation.name: Overworld Encounter Gameplay
scope: Party interaction and pressure-driven encounter generation
Strategic outcomes routed into mission/tactical layers
Order-to-outcome feedback in UI and event logs

phasedImplementation.name: Economy and Logistics
scope: Supply, recruitment, reinforcement, and garrison loops
Order costs and constraints
Initial balancing pass for multi-party viability

phasedImplementation.name: Tactical Bridge and Scene Continuity
scope: Party-driven entry into tactical/local scenes
Non-focused party simulation while one party is directly controlled
Improved overworld -> region -> local continuity

phasedImplementation.name: Character Creation and Determinism
scope: Faction and backstory flow with recruit-bias effects
Save migration guards for added campaign fields
Determinism and regression fixtures for party simulation and transitions

stories.title: Complete party state foundation and persistence
stories.description: As a developer, I want campaign party state fully defined and persisted so multi-party gameplay survives save/load without ambiguity.
acceptanceCriteria: Campaign party state includes selected party, leader mapping, order kind, current/target region, and notice fields.
Save/load serializes and restores all party fields required by the model without lossy fallback.
Example: after saving with one delegated party on Patrol targeting Region-B, loading restores Patrol + Region-B target exactly.
Negative case: loading a save missing party fields yields a compatibility failure status and avoids partial corrupted runtime state.
`cargo test` passes including tests that cover party state round-trip serialization.

stories.title: Enforce start-menu action hierarchy and continue semantics
stories.description: As a player, I want a clear start menu where New Campaign is primary and Continue resumes correctly so onboarding is predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Start menu prioritizes New Campaign visually and interaction-wise over other actions.
Continue Campaign resumes latest compatible save/autosave into correct campaign context instead of unrelated screens.
Saves diagnostics remain collapsed by default and do not dominate first-run readability.
Example: with a valid autosave, selecting Continue enters saved overworld context in one action.
Negative case: with no compatible saves, Continue shows explicit failure status and remains on start menu.
`cargo test` passes including tests for continue-selection behavior with and without compatible saves.

stories.title: Isolate menu subtitle/status text from in-session notices
stories.description: As a player, I want start-menu messaging separated from runtime notices so the menu context remains clear and stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Start-menu subtitle source is decoupled from hub/campaign notice channels.
In-session action notices no longer appear on start menu after returning to it.
Example: completing a mission action notice, then returning to menu, shows menu-appropriate subtitle copy only.
Negative case: stale hub notice text must not appear as start-menu subtitle under any menu re-entry path.
`cargo test` passes with coverage for state-source isolation between menu and runtime notices.

stories.title: Implement character creation step 1 (faction selection)
stories.description: As a player, I want to choose a faction after starting a new campaign so campaign context and bonuses are explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: New Campaign transitions to Character Creation faction screen before entering overworld.
Faction choices display clear gameplay impact text before confirmation.
Confirmed faction selection is stored in campaign state for subsequent steps and save/load.
Example: selecting Faction-X applies expected faction identifier in campaign state and advances to backstory step.
Negative case: attempting to continue without selecting a faction blocks progression with actionable UI feedback.
`cargo test` passes with flow tests for faction selection persistence and gating.

stories.title: Implement character creation step 2 (backstory/archetype effects)
stories.description: As a player, I want backstory selection with explicit effects so my campaign start identity changes gameplay outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Backstory screen lists archetypes with stat modifiers and recruit-generation bias details.
Backstory confirmation writes modifiers to player/campaign state and marks character creation complete.
Completion transitions into overworld map using same campaign seed.
Example: selecting Scout archetype applies expected scouting/recruit bias modifiers and enters overworld.
Negative case: invalid or missing backstory identifier cannot be committed and shows recoverable validation message.
`cargo test` passes including tests that verify modifier application and completion flags.

stories.title: Deliver party list control UX with command handoff
stories.description: As a player, I want clear party selection and command handoff controls so I can manage multiple parties intentionally.
acceptanceCriteria: Overworld panel lists all parties with clear markers for selected and directly controlled parties.
Take Command action transfers direct control to eligible delegated party and updates focus context.
Party order and notice information is visible and updates after actions.
Example: selecting delegated Party-B then Take Command makes Party-B controlled and previously controlled party delegated.
Negative case: Take Command on invalid/ineligible party is blocked with explicit reason and no state mutation.
`cargo test` passes with tests for command handoff invariants and UI state bindings.

stories.title: Implement explicit region target picker workflow
stories.description: As a player, I want map-click region target assignment for delegated orders so order intent is precise and discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Region target picker supports enter-select-confirm-cancel flow for delegated order assignment.
Chosen target region is displayed in party panel and used by delegated movement.
Picker state is visually distinct from normal selection interactions.
Example: assign Patrol to Region-C through picker and observe party target state update to Region-C.
Negative case: confirming without a valid region selection is rejected and preserves previous target.
`cargo test` passes including interaction tests for picker state transitions and target persistence.

stories.title: Add camera focus interpolation for party swaps
stories.description: As a player, I want smooth camera focus transitions when switching controlled parties so orientation remains understandable.
acceptanceCriteria: Party focus action interpolates camera toward selected/controlled party rather than snapping abruptly.
Focus transition locks conflicting camera commands until interpolation completion or safe interrupt path.
UI reflects transition state to avoid ambiguous input outcomes.
Example: hand off control to Party-B triggers smooth move and finishes with Party-B centered and selected region synced.
Negative case: repeated rapid focus actions do not leave camera in undefined state or desync selected party.
`cargo test` passes with deterministic tests for interpolation state machine behavior.

stories.title: Implement overworld-to-region transition payload
stories.description: As a player, I want explicit region entry from overworld so progression into regional gameplay is intentional and deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Overworld exposes explicit region-entry action tied to selected region context.
Transition payload includes region id, faction context, and deterministic seed data needed by region layer.
During transition, interaction is state-locked to prevent dual-layer input conflicts.
Example: entering Region-D loads region scene with Region-D id and expected campaign seed-derived context.
Negative case: entering region with missing payload fields fails gracefully and returns to overworld with clear status.
`cargo test` passes with payload contract tests and transition-guard tests.

stories.title: Bootstrap region-to-local eagle-eye intro sequence
stories.description: As a player, I want the local scene to start with my character exiting a dilapidated building so campaign immersion matches the intended narrative start.
acceptanceCriteria: Region layer can trigger local eagle-eye scene bootstrap with selected region context.
Local intro places dilapidated building anchor and spawns player character initially hidden/inside.
Intro sequence animates character exit and then hands control to normal gameplay input.
Example: entering local intro from Region-D shows building anchor, exit animation, then controllable player state.
Negative case: if anchor prefab/geometry is unavailable, bootstrap aborts safely with recoverable status and no hard crash.
`cargo test` passes including tests for intro completion flag and input handoff state changes.

stories.title: Persist layered campaign progression and migration guards
stories.description: As a returning player, I want saves to resume at the correct campaign layer with compatibility protection so progress remains stable across updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Save data includes character creation choices, current layer, region/local context, party state, and intro completion flag.
Load flow restores into menu, overworld, region, or local layer according to saved progression marker.
Migration checks detect incompatible versions and provide non-destructive fallback messaging.
Example: save during region layer then load resumes same region context with preserved selected party.
Negative case: incompatible version save is rejected with explicit compatibility status and no partial load side effects.
`cargo test` passes with migration and layer-resume coverage.

stories.title: Add deterministic regression fixtures and screenshot stages
stories.description: As a developer, I want deterministic fixtures and screenshot baselines for key campaign stages so regressions in flow and visual state are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: Deterministic fixtures cover start menu, character creation, overworld, region view, and local intro frame.
Screenshot stage capture includes dedupe behavior and golden-baseline verification for the listed stages.
Fixture execution under same seed yields stable state outputs required by regression assertions.
Example: running regression fixture with seed S-001 produces matching baseline captures across repeated runs.
Negative case: modified UI/state causing baseline mismatch fails regression checks with stage-specific diagnostics.
`cargo test` passes including deterministic fixture assertions and screenshot-stage validation hooks.
